titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Add. Ch. 66570

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=77 status=live modified=2018-11-28 15:20:35.603885+00 | translation moa=78 status=live modified=2018-11-28 15:22:14.478925+00

div.p:
Alexander Dei gratia Rex Scottorum . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue salutem. Sciatis quod concessimus
Galfrido de Ferseley . militi et fideli nostro . ut habeat et teneat terras suas quas de nobis tenet in feodo
de Carel’  in liberam Warennam . Quare firmiter prohibemus ne quis infra easdem terras sine Licencia eiusdem
Galfridi . secet aut uenetur . super nostram plenariam forisfacturam decem Libram . Testibus Roberto Abbate de Dum
fermelyn’ . Roberto de Meyners et Willelmo Olifard’ apud Dumfermelin .xvi. die Aprilis . Anno regni domini Regis  xxx quinto .

Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land greeting. Know that we have conceded to Geoffrey of Farsley, our knight and sworn man, that he shall have and hold his lands that he holds of us in feu at Crail in free Warren. Wherefore we strictly forbid that anyone may fell timber or hunt within the same lands without licence of the same Geoffrey, on pain of our full forfeiture of ten pounds. As witnesses: Robert, abbot of Dunfermline; Robert Menzies and William Oliphant. At Dunfermline, on the 16th day of April, in the 30th year of the reign of the lord King.
